title: Intel's new Core Ultra 200HX Plus mobile CPUs include 290HX, 270HX Plus flagships 8% faster gaming claims fall short of 15% boost promised by desktop Arrow Lake Refresh
summary: Intel's new Core Ultra 200HX Plus mobile CPUs have been released, with the 290HX offering an 8% gaming performance boost. The 270HX Plus is a flagship model, but its gaming performance metrics are currently unknown. This lags behind the promised 15% boost from the desktop Arrow Lake Refresh.
insight: Gap between mobile and desktop CPU performance claims widens.

title: Nvidia updates data center roadmap with Rosa CPU and stacked Feynman GPUs optical NVLink, Groq LPUs with NVFP4, and NVLink also on deck
summary: Nvidia has unveiled its 2026-2028 data center roadmap, featuring the Rosa CPU, Feynman GPU, and optical NVLinks, as well as the integration of Groq LPUs with NVFP4 and NVLink. This marks a significant advancement in data center technology, aiming to improve performance and efficiency. The roadmap highlights Nvidia's continued commitment to innovation and leadership in the field.
insight: Accelerating AI and HPC with next-gen data center hardware advancements

title: Jensen says Nvidia has received orders from Chinese customers for H200 GPUs, licenses from US gov't H200 manufacturing restarting
summary: Nvidia's China supply chain resumes after over a year of restrictions, with orders for H200 GPUs and US government approval for H200 manufacturing restarting.
insight: Nvidia's China supply chain restart boosts GPU availability globally.

title: Jensen Huang says gamers are 'completely wrong' about DLSS 5 Nvidia CEO responds to DLSS 5 backlash
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang defends DLSS 5 despite gamer backlash, saying developers maintain artistic control.
insight: Artistic control remains with developers despite AI-enhanced graphics.

title: Nvidia launches DGX Station with its bleeding-edge GB300 Grace Blackwell Superchip now available to order and will begin shipping in the coming months
summary: Nvidia launches DGX Station with the GB300 Grace Blackwell Superchip, offering 784GB of memory and a 1,600-watt power rating. This high-performance system is now available to order and will begin shipping in the coming months.
insight: Game-changing AI and HPC capabilities for enterprise customers

title: End of an era for decades-old PlayStation 3, Xbox 360, and Nintendo Wii U as GameStop officially declares them retro change means faulty or 'aesthetically unfortunate' consoles that can still power on are now accepted for trade-in
summary: GameStop declares PlayStation 3, Xbox 360, and Wii U retro consoles, accepting trade-ins of any console that powers on, even if faulty or aesthetically unappealing.
insight: Gamers can now trade in damaged consoles for store credit.

title: Nintendo Switch 2 update brings Handheld Mode Boost unlocks higher resolutions and TV-mode performance for original Switch games
summary: The Nintendo Switch 2's Handheld Mode Boost feature enhances original Switch games with improved visuals and performance, similar to docked mode, even when playing in handheld mode.
insight: Upgrade for original Switch games without need for re-releases.

title: Examining Nvidia's 60 exaflop Vera Rubin POD how seven chips underpin company's 40 rack AI factory supercomputer
summary: Nvidia announced seven chips for the Vera Rubin platform, set to be shipped in the second half of 2026. This platform will underpin the company's 40-rack AI factory supercomputer, a major breakthrough in AI computing. The Vera Rubin POD is expected to reach 60 exaflops.
insight: 60 exaflop AI supercomputer accelerates artificial intelligence research and innovation

title: We got a first look at Nvidia's DLSS 5 and the future of neural rendering at GTC the results can be impressive, but there's work to do
summary: Nvidia's DLSS 5 uses AI to enhance game visuals with detailed lighting interactions, producing impressive yet uncanny results.
insight: Realistic game lighting improves immersion and authenticity in gaming experiences

title: Microsoft expects to commercialize MicroLED datacenter cables by late 2027 expands Hollow Core Fiber deployment, promising 47% faster data transmission and approximately 33% lower latency
summary: Microsoft is developing MOSAIC, a MicroLED technology for datacenter cables. MOSAIC promises 47% faster data transmission and 33% lower latency. It uses MicroLEDs instead of lasers for data transmission.
insight: Revolutionizing data transmission with faster speeds and lower latency

title: Nvidia launches BlueField-4 STX storage architecture for agentic AI at GTC 2026
summary: Nvidia introduced BlueField-4 STX, a modular reference architecture for accelerated storage aimed at addressing the data access bottleneck in agentic AI inference. This architecture is designed to optimize data access and improve the performance of AI systems. It was announced at GTC 2026 on March 16.
insight: Accelerating data access boosts agentic AI inference performance significantly.

title: South Korean company following ChatGPT management advice forced to backtrack by court Krafton improperly ousted Subnautica developer bosses to dodge $250m bonus payout
summary: A South Korean gaming company was forced to backtrack after a court ruled that it improperly ousted the bosses of a developer to avoid a $250m bonus payment.
insight: AI-driven management decisions can have unintended legal consequences.

title: Airflow enthusiast 3D-prints 15 tiny fans to fit inside a custom, domed Noctua NF-A12x25 frame bizarre 'Fanhattan Project' cools the CPU just as well as a regular fan
summary: A YouTuber 3D-printed a custom fan with 15 tiny fans, achieving similar performance to a regular fan.
insight: Unconventional designs can match performance with less noise and bulk.

title: Global chip supply chain under threat as US-Iran conflict enters third week Strait of Hormuz blockade is days away from crippling Taiwan's semiconductor industry
summary: The US-Iran conflict threatens Taiwan's chip industry due to helium and LNG imports being disrupted. This is because Taiwan relies heavily on external supplies for both energy and essential materials. The resulting semiconductor shortage could have far-reaching global consequences.
insight: Disrupted imports imperil global chip supply chain resilience

title: Nvidia demonstrates Rubin Ultra tray, the world's first AI GPU with 1TB of HBM4E memory new chips will slot into Kyber racks
summary: Nvidia demonstrates the Rubin Ultra GPU with 1TB HBM4E memory, integrated into Kyber racks.
insight: Advances in AI computing with massive memory and compute integration.

title: Enthusiast reverse engineers world s smallest HDD to access data with help from an old Nokia N91 and OpenClaw open source USB bridge unlocks 0.85-inch mechanical Toshiba drive from 2004
summary: Maker Will Whang reverse engineers world's smallest HDD using an OpenClaw open source USB bridge, unlocking a 0.85-inch mechanical Toshiba drive from 2004, with the help of an old Nokia N91.
insight: Preserving vintage technology expands access to historical data and innovation.

title: Nvidia removes Rubin CPX accelerators from its roadmap Groq 3 LPUs take center stage as CPX is removed
summary: Nvidia has removed Rubin CPX accelerators from its roadmap, focusing on Groq 3 LPUs instead. The absence of CPX from Nvidia's GTC slides suggests a shift in priorities. This change may impact the field of AI acceleration.
insight: Nvidia prioritizes Groq LPUs over Rubin CPX accelerators now.

title: Seagate FireCuda X1070 2TB SSD review: Entry-level hardware meets premium support
summary: The Seagate FireCuda X1070 is a budget-friendly SSD that offers reliable performance and premium support. It's a Gen 4 SSD that's easy to install and use. However, its initial pricing might be a bit steep.
insight: Budget SSD meets premium support without breaking the bank

title: PycoClaw A MicroPython-based OpenClaw implementation for ESP32 and other microcontrollers
summary: PycoClaw is a MicroPython-based platform for AI agents on ESP32 and other microcontrollers, offering OpenClaw workspace compatibility, improved features, and support for multiple LLM providers.
insight: Enables OpenClaw-compatible AI on resource-constrained embedded devices with more features.

title: Review of Open Nextion ESP32-S3-based HMI displays with the Arduino IDE and the ESP-IDF framework
summary: ITEAD's Open Nextion ESP32-S3-based HMI displays combine logic processing, display control, and wireless connectivity into one package. Supported by the ESP-IDF framework and Arduino IDE with LVGL graphics, these HMI displays cater to developers and makers. They come in two variants with 3.5-inch and 2.8-inch screens.
insight: All-in-one ESP32-S3 HMI displays simplify IoT and GUI development

title: High-end 25MP global shutter camera with 10GbE interface is designed for NVIDIA Holoscan platform
summary: Leopard Imaging's LI-IMX530-10GigE-NL is a high-end 25MP camera designed for NVIDIA Holoscan. It features a 10GbE interface and is suitable for AI applications. The camera utilizes the Sony IMX530 1.2-inch CMOS sensor.
insight: Fast data transmission enables real-time AI and ML processing.

title: ADLINK DLAP-701 An NVIDIA Jetson T5000/T4000 Edge AI platform for humanoid robots and vision sensing systems
summary: ADLINK has launched the DLAP-701 Series, a compact edge AI platform for humanoid robots, autonomous mobile robots, and vision sensing systems, featuring NVIDIA Jetson T5000/T4000 processors, up to 128GB LPDDR5X memory, and various I/O options. It supports demanding edge environments with an operating voltage range of 9-36V DC and an industrial temperature range of -20 C to 60 C. The platform integrates CAN-FD interfaces for robotics and vehicle control and TPM 2.0 security.
insight: Enabling AI-driven robotics in harsh environments with robust performance

title: Samsung's Galaxy Z TriFold was a hit with buyers, but it's still shutting down after three months
summary: Samsung's Galaxy Z TriFold sold out quickly despite shutting down after three months. Limited production and tight control were key components of its initial strategy. Buyers were drawn to the experimental nature of the product.
insight: Limited production created hype, masking potential post-release issues.

title: MSI plans to raise prices by up to 30% amid memory crunch
summary: MSI plans to raise prices by 15-30% due to memory shortages.
insight: Memory scarcity drives price hikes across the PC hardware industry.

title: Nvidia unveils Vera, an 88-core Arm CPU for AI and analytics racks
summary: Nvidia unveiled Vera, an 88-core Arm CPU for AI and analytics. It focuses on general-purpose workloads with AI-centric applications. Vera is designed for data centers.
insight: Nvidia's Vera CPU targets broader AI workloads beyond GPU companionship

title: Startup proposes USB drives as a modern replacement for DVDs and Blu-rays
summary: Video StoreAge proposes using encrypted USB drives as a modern replacement for DVDs and Blu-rays, taking a more authorial approach to indie film distribution and disrupting algorithm-driven models.
insight: Human connection and community replace algorithm-driven film distribution methods.

title: I saw DLSS 5 running across multiple games. It's not a face filter.
summary: Nvidia released DLSS 5 at GTC 2026, sparking internet reactions.
insight: DLSS 5 enhances gaming performance across multiple supported titles.

title: Intel launches Core Ultra 200HX Plus "Arrow Lake Refresh" chips for gaming laptops
summary: Intel introduces Core Ultra 200HX Plus chips, led by the 9 290HX Plus, offering improved performance in gaming and professional applications.
insight: Increased single-threaded performance in gaming and professional applications with Intel Core Ultra 200HX Plus.

title: Jensen Huang predicts Nvidia AI chip revenue will hit $1 trillion by 2027
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang predicts $1 trillion AI chip revenue by 2027, marking a new stage in the AI boom where systems are designed for massive scale inference.
insight: AI chip revenue could surpass $1 trillion by 2027.

title: The PlayStation 5 is powered by a GPU roughly equal to which PC graphics card?
summary: The PlayStation 5's GPU is comparable to a high-end PC graphics card. It is considered to be roughly equivalent to a Radeon RX 6800 XT or a GeForce RTX 3070. This comparison is based on performance benchmarks.
insight: A console's GPU power determines its gaming capabilities and longevity.

title: User says Apple Watch caused severe wrist burns, case still unresolved six months later
summary: A Reddit user reported severe wrist burns from an Apple Watch, describing it as painful and slow to heal. The incident occurred while working on a laptop, causing an extreme surge of heat under the device. The user has been dealing with the issue for six months.
insight: Apple Watch safety concerns may lead to product liability lawsuits.

title: Amazon adds 1-hour and 3-hour delivery options, but convenience comes at a premium
summary: Amazon introduces 1-hour and 3-hour delivery options in major and smaller cities.
insight: Convenience comes with a higher price tag for Amazon customers.

title: Noctua's first PC case could be a quieter, fan-packed take on the Antec Flux Pro
summary: Noctua, an Austrian fan manufacturer, has shared a photo of its upcoming PC case. The case appears to be a quieter, fan-packed take on the Antec Flux Pro. The company provided a few details about its product in its replies to commentators.
insight: Noctua's first PC case could bring innovative cooling solutions

title: A London judge says a witness was being coached in real time through smart glasses
summary: A London judge accused a witness of being coached in real-time through smart glasses during a court case. The case involved a dispute over ownership and control of Oneta Limited. A Lithuanian firm and its representative were seeking a declaration and register rectification.
insight: Real-time coaching via smart glasses undermines court integrity and fairness

title: Data centers are turning to $200,000 robot dogs to guard the facilities
summary: Data centers are using expensive robot dogs for security patrols. Robot interest surged in the past year, driven by growing security needs. These robots can monitor facilities 24/7.
insight: Increased security demands drive adoption of autonomous robot guarding solutions.
